channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: The Midwest golf company that's changing the game in Sycamore, Illinois
item.description: Sub 70, a golf company based in Sycamore, Illinois, offers custom-made golf equipment and personal service to its customers, while maintaining its Midwest roots and high-quality craftsmanship.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: Briscoe gives Toyota its first Daytona 500 pole as Johnson and Truex also lock into NASCAR opener
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Chase Briscoe led a banner night for Toyota in qualifying for the Daytona 500 by winning the pole for NASCAR's “Great American Race.” It is the first Daytona 500 pole for Briscoe, who moved to Joe Gibbs Racing this season when Stewart-Haas Racing closed at the end of 2024.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nick Pivetta agrees to $55 million, 4-year contract with San Diego Padres, AP source says
item.description: Nick Pivetta became the last of the high-profile free agent pitchers to reach an agreement this offseason, a backloaded $55 million, four-year contract with the San Diego Padres, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Villanova edges No. 9 St. John's 73-71 to stop Red Storm's winning streak
item.description: Tyler Perkins hit a go-ahead 3-pointer with 8 seconds remaining and Villanova defeated No. 9 St. John’s 73-71, ending the Red Storm’s 10-game winning streak.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mets finalize Pete Alonso deal and re-sign injured reliever Drew Smith
item.description: Pete Alonso and the New York Mets have finalized their $54 million, two-year contract on the same day spring training opened with the team’s first formal workout for pitchers and catchers.
item.dc:creator: Mike Fitzpatrick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Baseball welcomes another season, with most pitchers and catchers reporting Wednesday
item.description: There's a new No. 2 in the New York Yankees' rotation behind Gerrit Cole. A familiar face is getting a fresh start leading the Cincinnati Reds. Meanwhile, Shohei Ohtani & Co. are preparing a title defense.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NBA All-Star weekend: What’s happening around Bay Area as basketball showcase arrives
item.description: From concerts to panels, this weekend's NBA All-Star event in Bay Area brings much more than dunks, 3-point shots to region.
item.dc:creator: Bay Area News Group, The Mercury News

item.title: Tim Walz's daughter rants against Trump's transgender athlete executive order
item.description: Tim Walz's daughter, Hope Waltz, made TikTok videos criticizing President Donald Trump's executive order banning transgender athletes from women's and girls sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trans athlete Sadie Schreiner not competing for RIT women's track team after Trump's executive order
item.description: Transgender track and field runner Sadie Schreiner is not competing in future women's track events for Rochester Institute of Technology amid a new NCAA policy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Denny Hamlin leads fast group of Toyotas in NASCAR's opening practice for the Daytona 500
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Denny Hamlin led a trio of Toyota drivers — the manufacturer claimed five of the top six spots — in Wednesday's opening practice for the Daytona 500.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Pirates 1B Spencer Horwitz to miss at least 6-8 weeks following wrist surgery
item.description: BRADENTON, Fla. (AP) — Newly acquired Pittsburgh Pirates first baseman Spencer Horwitz is expected to miss most of spring training after undergoing surgery on his right wrist.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Funeral service held for Virginia McCaskey
item.description: A private funeral service will be held for Virginia Halas McCaskey, the 102-year-old owner of the Chicago Bears, after a public memorial service held in Des Plaines on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, WGN 9

item.title: Pistons set franchise record with 42-point halftime lead in 132-92 romp over Bulls
item.description: Malik Beasley made seven 3-pointers and finished with 24 points, and the Detroit Pistons built a franchise-record, 42-point halftime lead on the way to a 132-92 romp over the Chicago Bulls on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Madsen scores 28, but Utes lose at Cincinnati
item.description: CINCINNATI (ABC4 Sports) - Despite 28 points from Gabe Madsen against his former team, the Runnin' Utes fell at Cincinnati Tuesday night, 85-75. Madsen, who began his college career with the Bearcats, made eight three-pointers on 22 attempts, in matching a season high point total.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Egor Demin leads BYU past West Virginia, 73-69
item.description: MORGANTOWN, W.V. (ABC4 Sports) - Freshman Egor Demin scored 16 points to lead BYU to a 73-69 win over West Virginia on Tuesday night. Kanon Catchings added 11 points for BYU (16-8, 7-6 Big 12). "Gutsy win by our guys," said BYU head coach Kevin Young.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: NBA All-Star 2025 tip-off party canceled due to weather
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The official NBA All-Star 2025 tip-off party, which was set to take place Thursday at Thrive City has been canceled due to weather. The event was due to take place between 5 p.m. and 9 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: BYU Athletic Director Tom Holmoe announces retirement after 20 years
item.description: According to the BYU, Holmoe will be retiring at the end of the 2024-2025 athletic season, after one of the "most successful football seasons" of BYU.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Eagles running back Saquon Barkley sets the NFL's season rushing record, including the playoffs
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Saquon Barkley set an NFL single-season rushing record in the Super Bowl, held his young daughter as a torrent of confetti fluttered around them and flashed a smile as he held a Philadelphia newspaper with “CHAMPS!” stamped above his photo.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: PHOTOS: Celebrities spotted at the Super Bowl
item.description: The players on the field Sunday at Super Bowl LIX are celebrities in their own right, but they're far from the only big names gathered in New Orleans for the Chiefs-Eagles matchup.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: McCallum wins all-around as Red Rocks beat Arizona
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Grace McCallum won the all-around with a score of 39.65 to lead the 4th-ranked Utah gymnastics team past #19 Arizona, 197.675 to 196.125.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Martinez scores 30 as USU beats Fresno State, 89-81
item.description: FRESNO, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - Ian Martinez scored 30 points and led Utah State to an 89-81 victory over Fresno State Friday night. Martinez, who made 10 of 14 shots from the field, tied a career high with eight rebounds for the Aggies (21-3, 11-2 Mountain West Conference).
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Raiders legend Fred Biletnikoff keeps daughter's dream alive
item.description: LOOMIS, Calif. - Fred Biletnikoff still goes to a lot of Raiders games. "Sometimes I don't know why," laughed the Hall of Fame wideout. "It's just the history." Fred has a lot of history with the Raiders.
item.dc:creator: Rick Tarsitano, WGN 9

item.title: Edwards scores 49 to lead Wolves over short-handed Bulls 127-108
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Anthony Edwards scored 49 points, including 20 in the third quarter, as the Minnesota Timberwolves beat the short-handed Chicago Bulls 127-108 on Wednesday night. Edwards hit six 3-pointers and was 17 of 18 from the free-throw line and also added nine rebounds.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Madsen brothers lead Utes past Colorado, 72-59
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Gabe Madsen scored 17 points, Lawson Lovering scored 12 points and Utah kept Colorado winless in the Big 12 beating the Buffaloes 72-59 on Wednesday night. Ezra Ausar and reserve Mason Madsen each scored 10 points for the Utes (13-9, 5-6 Big 12).
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
